text:
Erik loved flying. He had taken up long-distance aviation during college, scouring the globe collecting tales from daring aviators. Every hangar was a treasure trove of stories.

There was Jerry, who flew sheep into remote valleys. He spoke of rugged terrains and sheep that knew him better than he knew them.

Or Ted, who operated a courier service out of small airports. Flying his planes on weekends meant trading cities for landscapes painted vividly through blades.

Erik had his sights set on something grander. It was a dream to travel anonymously, visiting places unnamed in history, feeling the wind on his face as he discovered treasures untold.

One evening, he perched on the modest flightdeck of his biplane. Beneath the starlit dome, the world was a canvas of textures, from the snow-capped peaks to the warm, pulsing life of cloud forests.

He was about to fly from a sleepy coastal town into the unknown when a radio crackled.

"
